retrieved_result: Whatâs The Ventilatory Threshold, And Why Does It Matter? What’s The Ventilatory Threshold, And Why Does It Matter? By Matt Fitzgerald, Published Oct. 16, 2013Being out of breath either during or after a race is a part of every runner's life. Photo: www.shutterstock.com Labored breathing is very familiar to runners. They call it the ventilatory threshold.
Decision_Reason: The article discusses the ventilatory threshold, a physiological concept linked to endurance performance and training—relevant to improving athletic performance, though not a general 'how to get better at sports' guide.
